AbsolutePathBuf.description:
A path that is guaranteed to be absolute and normalized (though it is not
guaranteed to be canonicalized or exist on the filesystem).

IMPORTANT: When deserializing an `AbsolutePathBuf`, a base path must be set
using [AbsolutePathBufGuard::new]. If no base path is set, the
deserialization will fail unless the path being deserialized is already
absolute.

config_file.description:
Path to a role-specific config layer.
Relative paths are resolved relative to the `config.toml` that defines them.

description.description:
Human-facing role documentation used in spawn tool guidance.
Required unless supplied by the referenced agent role file.

nickname_candidates.description: Candidate nicknames for agents spawned with this role.

interrupt_message.description:
Whether to record a model-visible message when an agent turn is interrupted.
Defaults to true.

job_max_runtime_seconds.description: Default maximum runtime in seconds for agent job workers.

max_depth.description:
Maximum nesting depth allowed for spawned agent threads.
Root sessions start at depth 0.

max_threads.description:
Maximum number of agent threads that can be open concurrently.
When unset, no limit is enforced.

AltScreenMode.description:
Controls whether the TUI uses the terminal's alternate screen buffer.

- `auto` (default): Use alternate screen mode.
- `always`: Always use alternate screen mode.
- `never`: Never use alternate screen mode. Runs in inline mode, preserving scrollback.

The CLI flag `--no-alt-screen` can override this setting at runtime.

oneOf.description: Use alternate screen mode.
Always use alternate screen mode.
Never use alternate screen (inline mode only).

AnalyticsConfigToml.description: Analytics settings loaded from config.toml. Fields are optional so we can apply defaults.

enabled.description: When `false`, disables analytics across Codewith product surfaces in this profile.

AppConfig.description: Config values for a single app/connector.

approvals_reviewer.description: Reviewer for approval prompts from this app, overriding the thread default.

default_tools_approval_mode.description: Approval mode for tools in this app unless a tool override exists.

default_tools_enabled.description: Whether tools are enabled by default for this app.

destructive_enabled.description: Whether tools with `destructive_hint = true` are allowed for this app.

enabled.description: When `false`, Codewith does not surface this app.

open_world_enabled.description: Whether tools with `open_world_hint = true` are allowed for this app.

AppToolConfig.description: Per-tool settings for a single app tool.

enabled.description: Whether this tool is enabled. `Some(true)` explicitly allows this tool.

AppToolsConfig.description: Tool settings for a single app.

ApprovalsReviewer.description: Configures who approval requests are routed to for review. Examples include sandbox escapes, blocked network access, MCP approval prompts, and ARC escalations. Defaults to `user`. `auto_review` uses a carefully prompted subagent to gather relevant context and apply a risk-based decision framework before approving or denying the request. The legacy value `guardian_subagent` is accepted for compatibility.

AppsDefaultConfig.description: Default settings that apply to all apps.

destructive_enabled.description: Whether tools with `destructive_hint = true` are allowed by default.

enabled.description: When `false`, apps are disabled unless overridden by per-app settings.

open_world_enabled.description: Whether tools with `open_world_hint = true` are allowed by default.

AskForApproval.description:
Determines the conditions under which the user is consulted to approve
running the command proposed by Codewith.

oneOf.description:
Under this policy, only "known safe" commands—as determined by
`is_safe_command()`—that **only read files** are auto‑approved.
Everything else will ask the user to approve.

DEPRECATED: *All* commands are auto‑approved, but they are expected to
run inside a sandbox where network access is disabled and writes are
confined to a specific set of paths. If the command fails, it will be
escalated to the user to approve execution without a sandbox.
Prefer `OnRequest` for interactive runs or `Never` for non-interactive
runs.
The model decides when to ask the user for approval.

Fine-grained controls for individual approval flows.

When a field is `true`, commands in that category are allowed. When it
is `false`, those requests are automatically rejected instead of shown
to the user.

Never ask the user to approve commands. Failures are immediately returned
to the model, and never escalated to the user for approval.

AuthCredentialsStoreMode.description: Determine where Codewith should store CLI auth credentials.

oneOf.description: Persist credentials in CODEWITH_HOME/auth.json.
Persist credentials in the keyring. Fail if unavailable.
Use keyring when available; otherwise, fall back to a file in CODEWITH_HOME.
Store credentials in memory only for the current process.
Prefer the profile with the most remaining enabled Codewith limit.
Preserve configured order and rotate to the next available profile.

enabled.description:
Enables runtime switching to another auth profile when selected rate
limit windows are fully exhausted.

heartbeat_freshness_secs.description: Maximum age in seconds for usage data to guide profile selection.

heartbeat_interval_secs.description: Seconds between background usage heartbeat checks.

on_5h_limit.description: Switch when the 5h Codewith window reaches 100%.

on_weekly_limit.description: Switch when the weekly Codewith window reaches 100%.

profiles.description:
Preferred profile order. When empty, saved auth profiles are used in
sorted profile-name order.

strategy.description: Strategy used to choose the next profile.

AutoCompactTokenLimitScope.description:
Selects which part of the active context is charged against
`model_auto_compact_token_limit`.

oneOf.description: Count the full active context against the limit.
Count sampled output and later growth after the carried window prefix.

policy.description: Additional policy instructions inserted into the guardian prompt.

excluded_tool_namespaces.description: Exact tool namespaces to omit from the code-mode nested tool surface.

ConfigProfile.description:
Collection of common configuration options that a user can define as a unit
in `config.toml`.

features.description: Optional feature toggles scoped to this profile.

model_catalog_json.description: Optional path to a JSON model catalog (applied on startup only).

model_gateway.description: Gateway to use above the selected model provider.

model_instructions_file.description: Optional path to a file containing model instructions.

model_provider.description:
The key in the `model_providers` map identifying the
[`ModelProviderInfo`] to use.

service_tier.description:
Optional explicit service tier request id for new turns (for example
`default`, `priority`, or `flex`; legacy `fast` also works).

tui.description: TUI settings scoped to this profile.

allow_codex_version_mismatch.description: Allow replaying a lock generated by a different Codewith version.

export_dir.description: Directory where Codewith writes effective session config lock files.

load_path.description: Lockfile to replay as the authoritative effective config.

save_fields_resolved_from_model_catalog.description: Save fields resolved from the model catalog/session configuration.

ExternalConfigMigrationPrompts.description:
Settings for notices we display to users via the tui and app-server clients
(primarily the Codewith IDE extension). NOTE: these are different from
notifications - notices are warnings, NUX screens, acknowledgements, etc.

home.description: Tracks whether home-level external config migration prompts are hidden.

home_last_prompted_at.description: Tracks the last time the home-level external config migration prompt was shown.

project_last_prompted_at.description: Tracks the last time a project-level external config migration prompt was shown.

projects.description: Tracks which project paths have opted out of external config migration prompts.

enabled.description: When `false`, disables the feedback flow across Codewith product surfaces.

FileSystemAccessMode.description:
Access mode for a filesystem entry.

When two equally specific entries target the same path, we compare these by
conflict precedence rather than by capability breadth: `deny` beats
`write`, and `write` beats `read`.

oneOf.description: `none` is a legacy input alias retained temporarily for compatibility.

glob_scan_max_depth.description:
Optional maximum depth for expanding unreadable glob patterns on
platforms that snapshot glob matches before sandbox startup.

ForcedChatgptWorkspaceIds.description: Backward-compatible shape for ChatGPT workspace login restrictions in config.toml.

disable_warnings.description: Legacy no-op setting retained for compatibility.

ignore_large_untracked_dirs.description: Legacy no-op setting retained for compatibility.

ignore_large_untracked_files.description: Legacy no-op setting retained for compatibility.

auto_execute.description:
Controls whether goal plans may advance to ready goals without asking the
user between goals.

max_auto_goals_per_plan.description:
Maximum number of goals the model may add to one automatically executed
plan. Omit for the default cap.

max_tokens_per_goal_plan.description: Optional combined token cap for all goals in a plan. Omit for unlimited.

post_goal_context.description:
Context lifecycle action after a completed goal when no next goal is
immediately activated.

post_goal_plan_context.description: Context lifecycle action after a goal plan completes.

mcp_elicitations.description: Whether to allow MCP elicitation prompts.

request_permissions.description: Whether to allow prompts triggered by the `request_permissions` tool.

rules.description: Whether to allow prompts triggered by execpolicy `prompt` rules.

sandbox_approval.description:
Whether to allow shell command approval requests, including inline
`with_additional_permissions` and `require_escalated` requests.

skill_approval.description: Whether to allow approval prompts triggered by skill script execution.

History.description: Settings that govern if and what will be written to `~/.codewith/history.jsonl`.

max_bytes.description:
If set, the maximum size of the history file in bytes. The oldest entries
are dropped once the file exceeds this limit.

persistence.description: If true, history entries will not be written to disk.

oneOf.description: Save all history entries to disk.
Do not write history to disk.

KeybindingSpec.description:
Normalized string representation of a single key event (for example `ctrl-a`).

The parser accepts a small alias set (for example `escape` -> `esc`,
`pageup` -> `page-up`) and stores the canonical form.

This deliberately represents one terminal key event, not a sequence of
events. A value like `ctrl-x ctrl-s` is not a chord in this schema; adding
multi-step chords would require a separate runtime state machine.

KeybindingsSpec.description:
One action binding value in config.

This accepts either:

1. A single key spec string (`"ctrl-a"`).
2. A list of key spec strings (`["ctrl-a", "alt-a"]`).

An empty list explicitly unbinds the action in that scope. Because an
explicit empty list is still a configured value, runtime resolution must not
fall through to global or built-in defaults for that action.

last_revision.description: Git revision Codewith last successfully activated for this marketplace.

last_updated.description: Last time Codewith successfully added or refreshed this marketplace.

ref.description: Git ref to check out when `source_type` is `git`.

source.description: Source location used when the marketplace was added.

source_type.description: Source kind used to install this marketplace.

sparse_paths.description: Sparse checkout paths used when `source_type` is `git`.

McpServerOAuthConfig.description: OAuth client settings used when Codewith launches an MCP OAuth flow.

client_id.description: Explicit OAuth client identifier to present during authorization and token exchange.

McpServerToolConfig.description: Per-tool approval settings for a single MCP server tool.

dedicated_tools.description: When `true`, expose dedicated memory tools through the extension tool surface.

disable_on_external_context.description: When `true`, external context sources mark the thread `memory_mode` as `"polluted"`.

generate_memories.description: When `false`, newly created threads are stored with `memory_mode = "disabled"` in the state DB.

max_raw_memories_for_consolidation.description: Maximum number of recent raw memories retained for global consolidation.

max_rollout_age_days.description: Maximum age of the threads used for memories.

max_rollouts_per_startup.description: Maximum number of rollout candidates processed per pass.

max_unused_days.description: Maximum number of days since a memory was last used before it becomes ineligible for phase 2 selection.

min_rate_limit_remaining_percent.description: Minimum remaining percentage required in Codewith rate-limit windows before memory startup runs.

min_rollout_idle_hours.description: Minimum idle time between last thread activity and memory creation (hours). > 12h recommended.

use_memories.description: When `false`, skip injecting memory usage instructions into developer prompts.

ModelProviderAuthInfo.description: Configuration for obtaining a provider bearer token from a command.

command.description: Command to execute. Bare names are resolved via `PATH`; paths are resolved against `cwd`.

cwd.description: Working directory used when running the token command.

refresh_interval_ms.description:
Maximum age for the cached token before rerunning the command.
Set to `0` to disable proactive refresh and only rerun after a 401 retry path.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time to wait for the token command to exit successfully.

ModelProviderAwsAuthInfo.description: AWS SigV4 auth configuration for a model provider.

profile.description: AWS profile name to use. When unset, the AWS SDK default chain decides.

region.description: AWS region to use for provider-specific endpoints.

ModelProviderInfo.description: Serializable representation of a provider definition.

auth.description: Command-backed bearer-token configuration for this provider.

aws.description: AWS SigV4 auth configuration for this provider.

base_url.description: Base URL for the provider's OpenAI-compatible API.

env_http_headers.description:
Optional HTTP headers to include in requests to this provider where the
(key, value) pairs are the header name and _environment variable_ whose
value should be used. If the environment variable is not set, or the
value is empty, the header will not be included in the request.

env_key.description: Environment variable that stores the user's API key for this provider.

env_key_instructions.description:
Optional instructions to help the user get a valid value for the
variable and set it.

experimental_bearer_token.description:
Value to use with `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Use of this
config is discouraged in favor of `env_key` for security reasons, but
this may be necessary when using this programmatically.

http_headers.description:
Additional HTTP headers to include in requests to this provider where
the (key, value) pairs are the header name and value.

query_params.description: Optional query parameters to append to the base URL.

request_max_retries.description: Maximum number of times to retry a failed HTTP request to this provider.

requires_openai_auth.description:
Does this provider require an OpenAI API Key or ChatGPT login token? If true,
user is presented with login screen on first run, and login preference and token/key
are stored in auth.json. If false (which is the default), login screen is skipped,
and API key (if needed) comes from the "env_key" environment variable.

stream_idle_timeout_ms.description:
Idle timeout (in milliseconds) to wait for activity on a streaming response before treating
the connection as lost.

stream_max_retries.description: Number of times to retry reconnecting a dropped streaming response before failing.

supports_websockets.description: Whether this provider supports the Responses API WebSocket transport.

websocket_connect_timeout_ms.description:
Maximum time (in milliseconds) to wait for a websocket connection attempt before treating
it as failed.

wire_api.description: Which wire protocol this provider expects.

external_config_migration_prompts.description: Tracks scopes where external config migration prompts should be suppressed.

fast_default_opt_out.description: Tracks whether the user opted out of Codewith-managed fast defaults.

hide_full_access_warning.description: Tracks whether the user has acknowledged the full access warning prompt.

hide_gpt-5.1-codex-max_migration_prompt.description: Tracks whether the user has seen the gpt-5.1-codex-max migration prompt

hide_gpt5_1_migration_prompt.description: Tracks whether the user has seen the model migration prompt

hide_rate_limit_model_nudge.description: Tracks whether the user opted out of the rate limit model switch reminder.

hide_world_writable_warning.description: Tracks whether the user has acknowledged the Windows world-writable directories warning.

model_migrations.description: Tracks acknowledged model migrations as old->new model slug mappings.

oneOf.description: Emit TUI notifications only while the terminal is unfocused.
Emit TUI notifications regardless of terminal focus.

OAuthCredentialsStoreMode.description: Determine where Codewith should store and read MCP credentials.

oneOf.description:
`Keyring` when available; otherwise, `File`.
Credentials stored in the keyring will only be readable by Codewith unless the user explicitly grants access via OS-level keyring access.

CODEWITH_HOME/.credentials.json
This file will be readable to Codewith and other applications running as the same user.
Keyring when available, otherwise fail.

OtelConfigToml.description: OTEL settings loaded from config.toml. Fields are optional so we can apply defaults.

environment.description: Mark traces with environment (dev, staging, prod, test). Defaults to dev.

span_attributes.description: Attributes to add to every exported trace span.

tracestate.description: Semicolon-separated `key:value` fields to upsert into W3C tracestate members.

oneOf.description: Binary payload
JSON payload

mcp_servers.description: Per-MCP-server policy overlays for MCP servers contributed by this plugin.

PluginMcpServerConfig.description:
Policy settings for a plugin-provided MCP server.

This intentionally excludes transport settings: plugin manifests own how the
MCP server is launched, while user config owns enablement and tool policy.

default_tools_approval_mode.description: Approval mode for tools in this server unless a tool override exists.

disabled_tools.description: Explicit deny-list of tools. These tools are removed after applying `enabled_tools`.

enabled.description: When `false`, Codewith skips initializing this plugin MCP server.

enabled_tools.description: Explicit allow-list of tools exposed from this server.

tools.description: Per-tool approval settings keyed by tool name.

PrivacyFilterConfigToml.description:
Optional local Privacy Filter secret-redaction layer.

Opt-in and off by default. This slice only exposes the toggle plus a local
readiness check; the on-device classifier install and composer redaction are
tracked as follow-up work.

enabled.description: Master gate for the Privacy Filter. Defaults to `false` (disabled).

ProfileTui.description: TUI settings supported inside a named profile.

session_picker_view.description: Preferred layout for resume/fork session picker results.

RawMcpServerConfig.description:
Raw MCP config shape used for deserialization and supported-field JSON
Schema generation.

Fields that are accepted only to produce targeted validation errors should
be skipped in the generated schema.

Keep `TryFrom<RawMcpServerConfig> for McpServerConfig` exhaustively
destructuring this struct so new TOML fields cannot be added here without
updating the validation/mapping logic that produces [`McpServerConfig`].

name.description: Legacy display-name field accepted for backward compatibility.

ReasoningEffort.description: A non-empty reasoning effort value advertised by the model.

ReasoningSummary.description:
A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be useful for
debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process.
See https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning?api-mode=responses#reasoning-summaries

oneOf.description: Option to disable reasoning summaries.

SessionPickerViewMode.description: Preferred layout for the resume/fork session picker.

enabled.description: Enables background session recaps in interactive TUI sessions.

fallback_model.description: Fallback model used when the preferred recap model is unavailable.

idle_minutes.description:
Minutes the terminal must stay unfocused after the last completed turn
before Codewith prepares an automatic recap.

model.description: Preferred lightweight model for recap generation.

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort requested for recap generation.

oneOf.description:
"Core" environment variables for the platform. On UNIX, this would
include HOME, LOGNAME, PATH, SHELL, and USER, among others.
Inherits the full environment from the parent process.
Do not inherit any environment variables from the parent process.

ShellEnvironmentPolicyToml.description: Policy for building the `env` when spawning a process via shell-like tools.

include_instructions.description: Whether turns receive the automatic skills instructions block.

enabled.description: Enables the experimental pre-tool advisory pass. Defaults to false.

max_input_chars.description: Maximum serialized tool input characters exposed to the advisory model.

max_suggestion_chars.description: Maximum advisory text characters injected back into the main context.

model.description: Required model id for the advisory pass when enabled.

model_provider.description: Optional configured model provider id used for the advisory pass.

service_tier.description: Optional service tier forwarded only to the advisory model request.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time to wait for advisory output, in milliseconds.

ToolPolicy.description:
Hardened tool-exposure policy for the model toolset.

Selecting a policy switches Codewith into a native AuthCapsule enforcement
mode. This is a first-class configuration key so that a `--strict-config`
launch accepts `-c tools.policy="infinity-agent"` (or `[tools] policy =
"infinity-agent"`) instead of rejecting it as an unknown field.

oneOf.description: Default policy. Preserves the complete ordinary Codewith tool surface.

Infinity subscription AuthCapsule policy. Removes host filesystem tools,
host shell tools, and auth-profile control from the model toolset and
mediates every surviving tool call through the protected remote-tool
bridge. See `Config::infinity_agent_policy` and the tool planner for
enforcement.

policy.description:
Selects the tool-construction policy. The default preserves the complete
ordinary Codewith tool surface. `None`/`Some(ToolPolicy::Full)` preserve
default tool exposure; `Some(ToolPolicy::InfinityAgent)` selects the
Infinity AuthCapsule `infinity-agent` policy.

TrustLevel.description:
Represents the trust level for a project directory.
This determines the approval policy and sandbox mode applied.

Tui.description: Collection of settings that are specific to the TUI.

alternate_screen.description:
Controls whether the TUI uses the terminal's alternate screen buffer.

- `auto` (default): Use alternate screen.
- `always`: Always use alternate screen.
- `never`: Never use alternate screen (inline mode only, preserves scrollback).

animations.description:
Enable animations (welcome screen, shimmer effects, spinners).
Defaults to `true`.

keymap.description:
Keybinding overrides for the TUI.

This supports rebinding selected actions globally and by context.
Context bindings take precedence over `global` bindings.

message_summary.description:
Ordered list of final assistant-message summary item identifiers.

When set, the TUI renders the selected items after completed assistant
messages. When unset, the TUI defaults to the full built-in summary.

model_availability_nux.description: Startup tooltip availability NUX state persisted by the TUI.

notification_condition.description:
Controls whether TUI notifications are delivered only when the terminal is unfocused or
regardless of focus. Defaults to `unfocused`.

notification_method.description:
Notification method to use for terminal notifications.
Defaults to `auto`.

notifications.description:
Enable desktop notifications from the TUI.
Defaults to `true`.

pet.description:
Pet id to preselect in the terminal pet picker.

Custom pet ids resolve against CODEWITH_HOME/pets/<pet-id>/pet.json.

pet_anchor.description:
Where the terminal pet should anchor vertically.

Defaults to `composer`, which follows the current TUI composer viewport.

raw_output_mode.description:
Start the TUI in raw scrollback mode for copy-friendly transcript output.
Defaults to `false`.

session_picker_view.description: Preferred layout for resume/fork session picker results.

show_tooltips.description:
Show startup tooltips in the TUI welcome screen.
Defaults to `true`.

status_line.description:
Ordered list of status line item identifiers.

When set, the TUI renders the selected items as the status line.
When unset, the TUI defaults to: `model-with-reasoning` and `current-dir`.

status_line_use_colors.description:
Color status line items with colors derived from the active syntax theme.
Defaults to `true`.

terminal_resize_reflow_max_rows.description:
Trim terminal resize-reflow replay to the most recent rendered terminal rows when the
transcript exceeds this cap. Omit to use Codewith's terminal-specific default. Set to `0` to
keep all rendered rows.

terminal_title.description:
Ordered list of terminal title item identifiers.

When set, the TUI renders the selected items into the terminal window/tab title.
When unset, the TUI defaults to: `activity` and `project`.
The `activity` item spins while working and shows an action-required
message when blocked on the user.

theme.description:
Syntax highlighting theme name (kebab-case).

When set, overrides automatic light/dark theme detection.
Use `/theme` in the TUI or see `$CODEWITH_HOME/themes` for custom themes.

vim_mode_default.description:
Start the composer in Vim mode (`Normal`) by default.
Defaults to `false`.

approve_for_prefix.description: Approve with exec-policy prefix when that option exists.

approve_for_session.description: Approve for session when that option exists.

open_fullscreen.description: Open the full-screen approval details view.

open_thread.description: Open the thread that requested approval when shown from another thread.

edit_queued_message.description: Edit the most recently queued message.

TuiComposerKeymap.description: Composer context keybindings. These override corresponding `global` actions.

history_search_next.description: Move to the next match in reverse history search.

history_search_previous.description: Open reverse history search or move to the previous match.

queue.description: Queue the current composer draft while a task is running.

TuiEditorKeymap.description: Editor context keybindings for text editing inside text areas.

delete_backward.description: Delete one grapheme to the left.

delete_forward.description: Delete one grapheme to the right.

insert_newline.description: Insert a newline in the editor.

kill_line_end.description: Kill text from cursor to line end.

kill_line_start.description: Kill text from cursor to line start.

move_down.description: Move cursor down one visual line.

move_left.description: Move cursor left by one grapheme.

move_line_end.description: Move cursor to end of line.

move_line_start.description: Move cursor to beginning of line.

move_right.description: Move cursor right by one grapheme.

move_up.description: Move cursor up one visual line.

move_word_left.description: Move cursor to beginning of previous word.

move_word_right.description: Move cursor to end of next word.

TuiGlobalKeymap.description: Global keybindings. These are used when a context does not define an override.

copy.description: Copy the last agent response to the clipboard.

cycle_permissions.description: Cycle to the next permissions preset.

open_external_editor.description: Open the external editor for the current draft.

queue.description: Queue the current composer draft while a task is running.

toggle_raw_output.description: Toggle raw scrollback mode for copy-friendly transcript selection.

toggle_vim_mode.description: Toggle Vim mode for the composer input.

TuiKeymap.description:
Raw keymap configuration from `[tui.keymap]`.

Each context contains action-level overrides. Missing actions inherit from
built-in defaults, and selected chat/composer actions can fall back
through `global` during runtime resolution.

This type is intentionally a persistence shape, not the structure used by
input handlers. Runtime consumers should resolve it into
`RuntimeKeymap` first so precedence, empty-list unbinding, and duplicate-key
validation are applied consistently.

TuiListKeymap.description: List selection context keybindings for popup-style selectable lists.

jump_bottom.description: Jump to the last list item.

jump_top.description: Jump to the first list item.

move_left.description: Move horizontally left in list pickers that support horizontal actions.

move_right.description: Move horizontally right in list pickers that support horizontal actions.

page_down.description: Move list selection down by one page.

page_up.description: Move list selection up by one page.

TuiPagerKeymap.description: Pager context keybindings for transcript and static overlays.

close_transcript.description: Close the transcript overlay via its dedicated toggle key.

half_page_down.description: Scroll down by half a page.

half_page_up.description: Scroll up by half a page.

oneOf.description: Anchor the pet to the bottom of the current TUI composer viewport.
Anchor the pet to the physical bottom of the terminal screen.

TuiVimNormalKeymap.description:
Vim normal-mode keybindings for modal editing inside text areas.

Actions that use uppercase letters (like `A` for append-line-end) should
be specified as `shift-a` in config; the runtime matcher handles
cross-terminal shift-reporting differences automatically.

append_line_end.description: Enter insert mode at end of line (`A`).

cancel_operator.description: Cancel a pending operator and return to normal mode.

change_to_line_end.description: Change from cursor to end of line and enter insert mode (`C`).

delete_to_line_end.description: Delete from cursor to end of line (`D`).

insert_line_start.description: Enter insert mode at first non-blank of line (`I`).

move_down.description: Move cursor down (`j`), or recall newer composer history at history boundaries.

move_line_end.description: Move cursor to end of line (`$`).

move_line_start.description: Move cursor to start of line (`0`).

move_up.description: Move cursor up (`k`), or recall older composer history at history boundaries.

move_word_backward.description: Move cursor to start of previous word (`b`).

move_word_end.description: Move cursor to end of current/next word (`e`).

move_word_forward.description: Move cursor to start of next word (`w`).

open_line_above.description: Open a new line above and enter insert mode (`O`).

open_line_below.description: Open a new line below and enter insert mode (`o`).

start_change_operator.description: Begin change operator; next keys select a text object.

start_delete_operator.description: Begin delete operator; next key selects motion (`d`).

start_yank_operator.description: Begin yank operator; next key selects motion (`y`).

substitute_char.description: Delete character under cursor and enter insert mode (`s`).

TuiVimOperatorKeymap.description:
Vim operator-pending keybindings for modal editing inside text areas.

This context is active only while waiting for a motion after `d` or `y`.
Repeating the operator key (`dd`, `yy`) targets the entire line. Pressing
`Esc` cancels the pending operator and returns to normal mode without
modifying text.

cancel.description: Cancel the pending operator and return to normal mode.

delete_line.description: Repeat delete operator to delete the whole line (`dd`).

motion_word_backward.description: Motion: to start of previous word (`b`).

motion_word_end.description: Motion: to end of current/next word (`e`).

motion_word_forward.description: Motion: to start of next word (`w`).

select_around_text_object.description: Select an around text object after an operator.

select_inner_text_object.description: Select an inner text object after an operator.

yank_line.description: Repeat yank operator to yank the whole line (`yy`).

TuiVimTextObjectKeymap.description: Vim text-object keybindings for modal editing inside text areas.

oneOf.description: Option to disable the URI-based file opener.

auto_reset_enabled.description:
When true, Codewith may consume one available reset credit after it confirms the weekly
usage limit is exhausted.

enabled.description: Enables automatic retry for recoverable usage and transient availability errors.

initial_backoff_secs.description: Initial retry delay in seconds for transient errors or usage errors without reset metadata.

max_backoff_secs.description: Maximum retry delay in seconds for exponential backoff.

max_reset_retry_delay_secs.description: Longest reset-based delay, in seconds, that Codewith will schedule automatically.

max_retries.description: Maximum automatic retry attempts per failing turn.

reset_retry_buffer_secs.description: Extra seconds to wait after a usage-limit reset timestamp before retrying.

Verbosity.description:
Controls output length/detail on GPT-5 models via the Responses API.
Serialized with lowercase values to match the OpenAI API.

sandbox_private_desktop.description:
Defaults to `true`. Set to `false` to launch the final sandboxed child
process on `Winsta0\\Default` instead of a private desktop.

WireApi.description: Wire protocol that the provider speaks.

oneOf.description: The Responses API exposed by OpenAI at `/v1/responses`.
The OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions API exposed at `/v1/chat/completions`.

mode.description: Controls whether this session class can use managed worktrees.

cleanup_default.description: Default cleanup policy when a managed worktree is released.

enabled.description: Global managed-worktree gate. When false, creation/attach actions should be unavailable.

root.description: Optional root directory for Codewith-owned managed worktrees.

sub_sessions.description: Policy for subagent and background-agent sessions.

allow_login_shell.description:
Whether the model may request a login shell for shell-based tools.
Default to `true`

If `true`, the model may request a login shell (`login = true`), and
omitting `login` defaults to using a login shell.
If `false`, the model can never use a login shell: `login = true`
requests are rejected, and omitting `login` defaults to a non-login
shell.

analytics.description:
When `false`, disables analytics across Codewith product surfaces in this machine.
Defaults to `true`.

approval_policy.description: Default approval policy for executing commands.

approvals_reviewer.description:
Configures who approval requests are routed to for review once they have
been escalated. This does not disable separate safety checks such as
ARC.

apps_mcp_product_sku.description: Optional product SKU forwarded on host-owned Codewith Apps MCP requests.

audio.description: Machine-local realtime audio device preferences used by realtime voice.

auth_profile_auto_switch.description: Runtime auth-profile failover behavior for rate-limited sessions.

auto_review.description: Optional policy instructions for the guardian auto-reviewer.

background_terminal_max_timeout.description:
Maximum poll window for background terminal output (`write_stdin`), in milliseconds.
Default: `300000` (5 minutes).

chatgpt_base_url.description: Base URL for requests to ChatGPT (as opposed to the OpenAI API).

check_for_update_on_startup.description:
When `true`, checks for Codewith updates on startup and surfaces update prompts.
Codewith is updated through explicit upstream merges and releases,
so this defaults to `false`.

cli_auth_credentials_store.description:
Preferred backend for storing CLI auth credentials.
file (default): Use a file in the Codewith home directory.
keyring: Use an OS-specific keyring service.
auto: Use the keyring if available, otherwise use a file.

compact_prompt.description: Compact prompt used for history compaction.

default_permissions.description:
Default permissions profile to apply. Names starting with `:` refer to
built-in profiles; other names are resolved from the `[permissions]`
table.

desktop.description: Opaque desktop settings stored alongside the rest of config.toml.

developer_instructions.description: Developer instructions inserted as a `developer` role message.

disable_paste_burst.description:
When true, disables burst-paste detection for typed input entirely.
All characters are inserted as they are received, and no buffering
or placeholder replacement will occur for fast keypress bursts.

experimental_realtime_start_instructions.description:
Experimental / do not use. Replaces the built-in realtime start
instructions inserted into developer messages when realtime becomes
active.

experimental_realtime_ws_backend_prompt.description:
Experimental / do not use. Overrides only the realtime conversation
websocket transport instructions (the `Op::RealtimeConversation`
`/ws` session.update instructions) without changing normal prompts.

experimental_realtime_ws_base_url.description:
Experimental / do not use. Overrides only the realtime conversation
websocket transport base URL (the `Op::RealtimeConversation`
`/v1/realtime`
connection) without changing normal provider HTTP requests.

experimental_realtime_ws_model.description:
Experimental / do not use. Selects the realtime websocket model/snapshot
used for the `Op::RealtimeConversation` connection.

experimental_realtime_ws_startup_context.description:
Experimental / do not use. Replaces the synthesized realtime startup
context appended to websocket session instructions. An empty string
disables startup context injection entirely.

experimental_smart_suggest.description:
Experimental / do not use. Opt-in advisory model pass before tool
execution for suggesting better tools or commands.

experimental_thread_config_endpoint.description:
Experimental / do not use. When set, app-server fetches thread-scoped
config from a remote service at this endpoint.

experimental_thread_store.description: Experimental / do not use. Selects the thread store implementation.

features.description: Centralized feature flags (new). Prefer this over individual toggles.

feedback.description:
When `false`, disables feedback collection across Codewith product surfaces.
Defaults to `true`.

file_opener.description:
Optional URI-based file opener. If set, citations to files in the model
output will be hyperlinked using the specified URI scheme.

forced_chatgpt_workspace_id.description: When set, restricts ChatGPT login to one or more workspace identifiers.

forced_login_method.description: When set, restricts the login mechanism users may use.

ghost_snapshot.description:
Compatibility-only settings retained so legacy `ghost_snapshot`
config still loads.

hide_agent_reasoning.description:
When set to `true`, `AgentReasoning` events will be hidden from the
UI/output. Defaults to `false`.

history.description: Settings that govern if and what will be written to `~/.codewith/history.jsonl`.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks configured inline in TOML plus user-level overrides.

include_apps_instructions.description: Whether to inject the `<apps_instructions>` developer block.

include_collaboration_mode_instructions.description: Whether to inject the `<collaboration_mode>` developer block.

include_environment_context.description: Whether to inject the `<environment_context>` user block.

include_permissions_instructions.description: Whether to inject the `<permissions instructions>` developer block.

log_dir.description:
Directory where Codewith writes log files. Setting this value explicitly
also enables the TUI text log in this directory.
Defaults to the Codewith home directory's `log` folder.

marketplaces.description: User-level marketplace entries keyed by marketplace name.

mcp_oauth_callback_port.description:
Optional fixed port for the local HTTP callback server used during MCP OAuth login.
When unset, Codewith will bind to an ephemeral port chosen by the OS.

mcp_oauth_callback_url.description:
Optional redirect URI to use during MCP OAuth login.
When set, this URI is used in the OAuth authorization request instead
of the local listener address. The local callback listener still binds
to 127.0.0.1 (using `mcp_oauth_callback_port` when provided).

mcp_oauth_credentials_store.description:
Preferred backend for storing MCP OAuth credentials.
keyring: Use an OS-specific keyring service.
         https://github.com/hasna/codewith/blob/main/codex-rs/rmcp-client/src/oauth.rs#L2
file: Use a file in the Codewith home directory.
auto (default): Use the OS-specific keyring service if available, otherwise use a file.

mcp_servers.description: Definition for MCP servers that Codewith can reach out to for tool calls.

model_auto_compact_token_limit.description: Token usage threshold triggering auto-compaction of conversation history.

model_auto_compact_token_limit_scope.description:
Controls whether the auto-compaction limit applies to the full context or
only to tokens after the carried prefix in the current compaction window.

model_catalog_json.description:
Optional path to a JSON model catalog (applied on startup only).
Per-thread `config` overrides are accepted but do not reapply this (no-ops).

model_context_window.description: Size of the context window for the model, in tokens.

model_gateway.description: Gateway to use above the selected model provider.

model_instructions_file.description:
Optional path to a file containing model instructions that will override
the built-in instructions for the selected model. Users are STRONGLY
DISCOURAGED from using this field, as deviating from the instructions
sanctioned by Codewith will likely degrade model performance.

model_provider.description: Provider to use from the model_providers map.

model_providers.description:
User-defined provider entries that extend the built-in list. Built-in
IDs cannot be overridden.

model_supports_reasoning_summaries.description: Override to force-enable reasoning summaries for the configured model.

model_verbosity.description: Optional verbosity control for GPT-5 models (Responses API `text.verbosity`).

notice.description:
Collection of in-product notices (different from notifications)
See [`crate::types::Notice`] for more details

notify.description: Optional external command to spawn for end-user notifications.

openai_base_url.description: Base URL override for the built-in `openai` model provider.

oss_provider.description: Preferred OSS provider for local models, e.g. "lmstudio" or "ollama".

personality.description: Optionally specify a personality for the model

plugins.description: User-level plugin config entries keyed by plugin name.

privacy_filter.description: Optional local Privacy Filter secret-redaction layer (opt-in).

profile.description: Profile to use from the `profiles` map.

profiles.description: Named profiles to facilitate switching between different configurations.

project_doc_fallback_filenames.description: Ordered list of fallback filenames to look for when default project instructions are missing.

project_doc_max_bytes.description: Maximum number of bytes to include from a project instruction file.

project_root_markers.description:
Markers used to detect the project root when searching parent
directories for `.codewith` folders. Defaults to [".git"] when unset.

realtime.description:
Experimental / do not use. Realtime websocket session selection.
`version` controls v1/v2 and `type` controls conversational/transcription.

review_model.description: Review model override used by the `/review` feature.

sandbox_workspace_write.description: Sandbox configuration to apply if `sandbox` is `WorkspaceWrite`.

service_tier.description:
Optional explicit service tier request id for new turns (for example
`default`, `priority`, or `flex`; legacy `fast` also works).

session_recap.description: Lightweight recap generation for returning to interactive sessions.

show_raw_agent_reasoning.description:
When set to `true`, `AgentReasoningRawContentEvent` events will be shown in the UI/output.
Defaults to `false`.

skills.description: User-level skill config entries keyed by SKILL.md path.

sqlite_home.description:
Directory where Codewith stores the SQLite state DB.
Defaults to `$CODEX_SQLITE_HOME` when set. Otherwise uses the Codewith home directory.

suppress_unstable_features_warning.description: Suppress warnings about unstable (under development) features.

tool_output_token_limit.description: Token budget applied when storing tool/function outputs in the context manager.

tool_suggest.description: Additional discoverable tools that can be suggested for installation.

tools.description: Nested tools section for feature toggles

tui.description: Collection of settings that are specific to the TUI.

usage_self_heal.description: Automatic retry behavior for recoverable usage-limit and availability failures.

web_search.description: Controls the web search tool mode: disabled, cached, or live.

worktrees.description: Managed worktree defaults and session-class gates.
